content.problemStatement:
You are given NNN edge-weighted trees T1,T2,…,TNT_1, T_2,\ldots, T_NT1​,T2​,…,TN​ having M1,M2,…,MNM_1, M_2,\ldots ,M_NM1​,M2​,…,MN​ nodes respectively. You also have an array AAA of N−1N-1N−1 positive integers.

You need to connect these NNN trees using N−1N-1N−1 edges such that each of these edges have weights equal to AiA_iAi​ for some i∈[1,N−1]i \in [1,N-1]i∈[1,N−1] and each of the AiA_iAi​ must be used exactly once. Note that after connecting these edges, a single large tree TTT will form consisting of (M1+M2+…+MN)(M_1+M_2+\ldots+M_N)(M1​+M2​+…+MN​) nodes. Find the minimum possible sum of distances between each pair of nodes in the final tree TTT.

Since this value can be large, output the value mod 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer NNN, denoting the number of trees.
Then, you are given the NNN trees. The description of each tree is as follows:

The first line contains the integer MiM_iMi​ (1≤i≤N)(1 \le i \le N)(1≤i≤N), the number of nodes in iii-th tree.
The next Mi−1M_i-1Mi​−1 lines describe the edges of the iii-th tree. The jjj-th of these Mi−1M_i-1Mi​−1 lines contains 333 space-separated integers uij,viju_{ij}, v_{ij}uij​,vij​ and wijw_{ij}wij​, describing that there is an edge of weight wijw_{ij}wij​ connecting the nodes uiju_{ij}uij​ and vijv_{ij}vij​ in tree TiT_iTi​.


The final line contains N−1N-1N−1 space-separated integers, A1,A2,…,AN−1A_1, A_2,\ldots,A_{N-1}A1​,A2​,…,AN−1​.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum sum of distances between each pair of nodes in tree TTT, modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
